findings.reachability: Reachable by any caller that passes a malicious archive (tar/zip/etc.) with crafted entry paths; the archive is the direct input to the exported module function.
findings.justification: The output-directory containment checks at lines 29 and 106 use `String.prototype.indexOf(realOutputPath) !== 0` instead of `startsWith(realOutputPath + path.sep)`, so a sibling directory whose name shares the same prefix (e.g. output='/tmp/out' and dest='/tmp/out-evil/...') satisfies the check and permits writes outside the intended output tree.

detection_pattern.description: Catches output-directory containment guards that use indexOf(prefix)===0 rather than startsWith(prefix+sep); the former is bypassable whenever a sibling path shares the same textual prefix.

findings.reachability: Reachable whenever the archive contains a symlink entry; no authentication or privilege is required to supply such an archive.
findings.justification: `fsP.symlink(x.linkname, dest)` is called with an archive-controlled `x.linkname` that is never validated; an attacker can create a symlink pointing to any absolute path (e.g. `/etc/passwd`), which persists in the output directory and allows subsequent reads of arbitrary files by the consuming application.

detection_pattern.description: Catches symlink creation calls that pass an archive-supplied `linkname` field directly without validating that it is relative and does not escape the output directory.

findings.reachability: Reachable whenever the archive contains a hard-link entry (tar type '1'); no authentication or privilege is required.
findings.justification: `fsP.link(x.linkname, dest)` is called with an archive-controlled `x.linkname` that is never validated; on systems where the output directory shares a filesystem with sensitive files (common for `/tmp` vs `/etc` on many Linux distros), an attacker can create a hardlink from within the output directory to any accessible file (e.g. `/etc/shadow`), exposing its contents.

detection_pattern.description: Catches hard-link creation calls that pass an archive-supplied `linkname` field directly without verifying the source path is within the allowed output tree.
